title: Probability Models and Stochastic Processes · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving reliability and arrival processes. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a reproducible stochastic models computational study while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate continuous random variables in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive joint conditional in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute limit theorems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
